pi_items.verified_value: Hybrid: Real iTunes Library data for media (artists, albums, tracks), synthetic customer/employee data, auto-generated sales data
pi_items.source: Verified from GitHub repository README and SQLite Tutorial documentation. Setup script (line 139) states 'Based on real iTunes Library data'. Web search confirmed media data is from real iTunes library, customer/employee data is fictional, sales data is randomly generated.

Direct inspection of downloaded Chinook database using sqlite3 '.schema' commands. The database schema uses singular names: Track, InvoiceLine, Album, Invoice, Customer, Artist, Genre, MediaType, Playlist, PlaylistTrack, Employee.
Direct SQL queries executed on downloaded Chinook database: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM Album/Track/Invoice/InvoiceLine/Customer

pi_items.verified_value: Full Album: Revenue 50.51, TransactionCount 49; Single/Partial: Revenue 2278.09, TransactionCount 1254
pi_items.source: Corrected SQL query executed on actual Chinook database (table names corrected from 'InvoiceItems'/'Tracks' to 'InvoiceLine'/'Track'). The setup_task.sh script contains table name errors that would cause query failure.

privileged_info_summary: The Chinook database is a well-known public sample database from GitHub (lerocha/chinook-database) used for SQL education and ORM testing. The database combines real iTunes Library data for media content with synthetic customer/employee information and auto-generated sales records. The task metadata correctly specifies paths for database file (/home/ga/Documents/databases/chinook.db), CSV output (/home/ga/Documents/exports/album_sales_analysis.csv), and SQL script (/home/ga/Documents/scripts/album_analysis.sql). The expected CSV headers (PurchaseType, TotalRevenue, TransactionCount) are correct. The Chinook database contains 347 albums, 3503 tracks, 412 invoices, 2240 invoice line items, and 59 customers. When the album completeness analysis query is properly executed (with corrected table names: 'Track' and 'InvoiceLine' instead of 'Tracks' and 'InvoiceItems'), it produces Full Album purchases (Revenue: 50.51, Count: 49) and Single/Partial purchases (Revenue: 2278.09, Count: 1254).
